data.body: SEQUENCING CORRECTION (grounded 2026-06-12): the bash test suites invoke $DISPATCHER = src/rebar/_engine/ticket (the bash dispatcher) DIRECTLY, not the argparse `rebar` console script. So porting a command to argparse in-process does NOT redirect the dispatcher suites — the bash impl (dispatcher arm + ticket-lib-api.sh function + any standalone .sh) stays live and exercised until the dispatcher AND its suites are retired together. Consequence: per-command bash DELETION is blocked until E7/E8. Revised flow for E2-E5 per command: (1) port to rebar.* reusing reducer/graph/resolver/output, (2) route argparse in-process (new in-process set), (3) rewire the library function off _run to in-process, (4) add a golden parity test pinning python==bash (the dual-run discipline; no kill-switch in Tier E). NO bash deletion in E2-E5. Deletion consolidates in E7 (dispatcher + engine_env machinery + ticket-lib*.sh + standalone gate .sh) and E8 (dispatcher suites -> pytest), sequenced so the dispatcher and the suites that call it retire together (E8 before/with E7). Each parity test is deleted with its bash second-impl in E7/E8 (the a93885e pattern).
